title: RNA Isolation from Plant Tissue Protocol 10: TRIzol LS Reagent Method
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"edgtb","text":"Implemented by: Michael Melkonian and Barbara Surek (algae) and Juan Carlos Villarreal (bryophytes)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7vpeu","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4av44","text":"This protocol follows the procedures provided with the TRIzol LS Reagent (Invitrogen). TRIzol LS Reagent is a monophasic solution of phenol and guanidine isothiocyanate that can be used in isolation of total RNA from a wide variety of tissues and organisms, in addition to plants. This protocol was used in the isolation of total RNA from some algae samples (see Supplementary Table 1).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4fak5","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7ipjv","text":"This protocol is part of a collection of eighteen protocols used to isolate total RNA from plant tissue. (RNA Isolation from Plant Tissue Collection: https://www.protocols.io/view/rna-isolation-from-plant-tissue-439gyr6) ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":150,"length":69}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"E965CAB9EED9477CA16F7416A8D9E427","url":"https://www.protocols.io/view/rna-isolation-from-plant-tissue-439gyr6"}}}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"37djr","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7nc54","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"5b1u8","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"notes","mutability":"IMMUTABLE","data":{"blocks":[{"data":[],"depth":0,"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"length":1,"offset":42}],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"683k9","text":"All centrifugation steps are performed at  .","type":"unstyled"},{"data":[],"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"6hsua","text":"","type":"unstyled"}],"entityMap":{"0":{"data":{"guid":"6F757830981711E9B236D9E4CF8D2A48","id":0,"label":"","protocol_name":"TRIzol LS Reagent Method","temperature":"4","unit":10},"mutability":"MUTABLE","type":"temperature"}},"guid":"42708E3AA98C4A119E529561E1C85C01"}}}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"cq0oi","text":"Please see SDS (Safety Data Sheet) for hazards and safety warnings.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Centrifuge at lowest speed to cause algae to form pellet.
Homogenize each 250 µL portion of pellet material to a powder in liquid nitrogen using mortar and pestle prechilled with liquid nitrogen.
Add 750 µL TRIzol LS to each 250 µL of homogenized algal material.
Homogenization is continued until the TRIzol is pulverized as well.
Thaw and aliquot homogenate into several Eppendorf tubes.
Add 50 µL potassium acetate ( 0.2 Molarity (M) final concentration) to each sample.
Incubate for 00:05:00 at 20 °C .
Add 200 µL chloroform (for polysaccharide-rich algae), or 100 µL BCP to each sample.
Incubate at 20 °C for 00:10:00 .
Centrifuge samples at for 00:15:00 .
RNA will remain in the upper, aqueous phase (ca. 70 % of the applied TRIzol).
Carefully transfer each RNA phase into RNase-free 1.5 ml tubes.
Add 500 µL isopropanol.
Incubate for 01:00:00 at -20 °C .
Centrifuge at for 00:10:00 .
Wash pellet with 75 % ethanol.
Gently suspend pellet in solution.
Centrifuge at for 00:05:00 .
Repeat ethanol wash steps. go to step #17
Dry pellet at 50 °C for 00:05:00 – 00:10:00 Appearance of drying pellet is important: drying should be terminated when the pellet begins to become transparent; contaminated RNA remains white)
Add RNAse-free water.
Dissolve pellet completely by pipetting.
Wash several times with sterile culture medium (not DEPC-treated).
After washing, the algal material is aliquoted into portions of 250 µL (ca. 50 mg – 100 mg packed cell volume).
Add more nitrogen if needed (see also Procedure described in Protocol 9).
Shake samples for 00:00:15 .
Incubate at 55 °C – 60 °C for 00:10:00 .
creator: Eric Carpenter
